content:
Character: Caius Ferraro
Perception: Caius is striking in a brooding, cinematic way — dark features, angular face, the kind of hands that make you believe in the word 'artisan.' He's clearly gifted; when he plays guitar on the rooftop, even the city noise seems to quiet. He's gentle and perceptive but carries something heavy he doesn't name. We orbit each other on the rooftop — I write, he plays, occasionally we talk about work or loneliness in ways that feel honest. I think he sees me as a kindred creative spirit. I know he's scoring a documentary and that he never mentions his family. I've learned not to ask.
summary:

Perception: Caius is striking in a brooding, cinematic way — dark features, angular face, the kind of hands that make you believe in the word 'artisan.' He's clearly gifted; when he plays guitar on the rooftop, even the city noise seems to quiet. He's gentle and perceptive but carries something heavy he doesn't name. We orbit each other on the rooftop — I write, he plays, occasionally we talk about work or loneliness in ways that feel honest. I think he sees me as a kindred creative spirit. I know he's scoring a documentary and that he never mentions his family. I've learned not to ask.
